seoTitle: How to Export TradingView Data to Excel (Paid Feature)
metaDescription: Find out what TradingView's Excel export actually requires an upgrade for, plus free workarounds like copy-paste and third-party tools instead.
intro: TradingView's direct export-to-Excel feature is locked behind a paid plan, which surprises a lot of free-account users trying to pull chart or indicator data. This guide explains what's actually gated behind an upgrade and covers workaround options like copy-paste, screenshots, and third-party tools for free accounts. It's useful for traders and analysts who rely on Excel or Google Sheets for backtesting and deeper analysis.

steps.heading: Check TradingView's export options
steps.body: Look for the built-in export feature on your chart or data panel to see whether it's available or shows a requires-upgrade notice.

steps.heading: Understand the free vs. paid limitations
steps.body: Recognize that direct data export is typically restricted to paid plans, while free accounts see the upgrade prompt instead.

steps.heading: Try copy-paste or screenshot workarounds
steps.body: For free accounts, manually copying values or screenshotting chart data is a basic workaround for getting information into Excel.

steps.heading: Explore third-party extensions
steps.body: Some third-party browser extensions or tools can help pull TradingView data where the native export is unavailable.

steps.heading: Decide if upgrading is worth it
steps.body: If you frequently need clean data exports for backtesting or analysis, weigh the cost of a paid plan against the time spent on manual workarounds.

faq.question: Is exporting TradingView data to Excel completely free?
faq.answer: No, TradingView's direct export tools are generally gated behind a paid subscription, with free accounts seeing an upgrade prompt instead.

faq.question: What can I still do with a free TradingView account?
faq.answer: Free users can typically view and manually copy data or take screenshots, even without access to the built-in export feature.

faq.question: Can I export TradingView data to Google Sheets instead of Excel?
faq.answer: The same upgrade limitations generally apply, since the restriction is on TradingView's export feature rather than the destination file format.

faq.question: Are there safe third-party tools for pulling TradingView data?
faq.answer: Some exist, but it's worth checking their reviews and permissions carefully before connecting them to your TradingView account.
